properties.titulo_evento: Poland buys V-BAT drones to boost Baltic Sea naval operations
properties.descricao: The deal puts Poland in line to become the first country on NATO’s eastern flank to acquire the Shield AI-made drone.

properties.descricao: The request includes $67.15 billion for the military, including $21 billion for munitions and to strengthen the U.S. industrial base.

properties.descricao: The firm showed a proposed battle tank at Eurosatory defense exhibition in Paris boasting a French turret and gun mounted on a German Leopard 2 chassis.

properties.titulo_evento: US Senate joins House in voting to halt Iran war

properties.descricao: Before the 1979 revolution in Iran, Washington and Tehran had close trade ties. Could a peace deal revive them?

properties.descricao: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

properties.descricao: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

properties.descricao: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine recovery summit opens in Poland without Zelensky
properties.descricao: A landmark conference on Ukraine's post-war reconstruction opened Thursday in Poland without Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, who is snubbing the event amid a diplomatic row with ally Warsaw.

properties.titulo_evento: UK swelters in hottest June day on record

properties.titulo_evento: Peru's Keiko Fujimori celebrates election win as opponent alleges fraud

properties.titulo_evento: Ethiopian journalists win US’ democracy award for courage

properties.titulo_evento: #Venezuela hit by deadly back-to-back #earthquakes

properties.titulo_evento: Germany: Racism is almost a daily reality for Muslims
properties.descricao: Insults, discrimination, violence, deaths: Experts warn of growing anti-Muslim racism in Germany — and criticize policymakers.

properties.titulo_evento: Fact check: No, Zelenskyy wasn't killed in a Russian airstrike

properties.titulo_evento: Europe heat wave: No respite as extreme heat continues
properties.descricao: Some 101 million people in Europe will likely see temperatures above 35 C on Thursday, an analysis shows. And Germany is expecting things to warm up even more. DW has the latest.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany news: Deutsche Bahn advises against travel amid heat
properties.descricao: Germany's national rail operator is allowing passengers to cancel trips because of the heat. Meanwhile, as Germany prepare for their next World Cup match, in-form striker Deniz Undav will stay on the bench. DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
properties.descricao: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran: Rubio says US rejects any nation's claim over Hormuz
properties.descricao: Marco Rubio says the US will not accept that the Strait of Hormuz belongs to any country. Meanwhile, Iran's Revolutionary Guard has warned against any crossings of Hormuz without authorization. Follow DW for more.

properties.titulo_evento: US: Trump feuds with Senators after rare Iran war rebuke
properties.descricao: US President Donald Trump abruptly canceled a housing bill signing ceremony and clashed with certain Senators after a rare motion seeking to rein in his war with Iran. The Senate passed a new motion following the feud.

properties.titulo_evento: Venezuela: 164 dead in devastating, back-to-back earthquakes
properties.descricao: The acting president has reported at least 164 deaths in Venezuela after two strong earthquakes rattled the capital and surrounding regions.

properties.titulo_evento: Venezuela hit by two powerful earthquakes
properties.descricao: Strong earthquakes struck west of Venezuela's capital just seconds apart, bringing down buildings in Caracas.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany's Merz rallies European NATO allies ahead of summit
properties.descricao: Chancellor Friedrich Merz hosted leaders of European NATO powers in Berlin to hash out consensus on bolstering European defense. With US tensions looming, the leaders signaled that transatlantic ties are improving.

properties.titulo_evento: What Keir Starmer's resignation means for European security
properties.descricao: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was widely known for his support of Ukraine and his efforts towards a new EU–UK defense partnership. What will happen to those policies now that Starmer has announced his resignation?

properties.titulo_evento: Climate change: Heat wave costs the German economy billions
properties.descricao: Never before has a June been as hot in Germany as in 2026. This has consequences not only for people and nature, but also for businesses. Heat reduces productivity and causes illness, while cooling is expensive.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
properties.descricao: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

properties.descricao: The White House is seeking more than $67 billion for the Pentagon and $11 billion for farm assistance.

properties.descricao: Increased stockpiles, more pipelines and other tactics could loosen the waterway’s stranglehold on the global economy.

properties.descricao: Oil prices fell, moving closer to levels not seen since the war in Iran began in February.
